patches.find: The observed Antarctic Oscillation (AAO) index averaged +0.78σ during the test period — roughly half the ≥+1.5σ threshold that would correspond to a ≥3% anomaly. The prediction failed by a factor of ~2.
patches.replace: The observed Antarctic Oscillation (AAO) index averaged +0.78σ during the test period (NCEP/CPC AAO index, week of 2026-03-12) — well below the extreme event that a ≥3% 500hPa anomaly in the 40–60°S band would require. The prediction clearly failed.
patches.rationale: Curmudgeon Cycle 2 (ISS-731): The '+1.5σ = ≥3% anomaly' equivalence is unjustified — AAO is defined on 700hPa anomalies south of 20°S, not 500hPa in the 40-60°S band. A +1.5σ AAO event produces ~0.8-1.6% height anomaly at 700hPa, far below 3%. Drop the conversion and report the raw failure directly. Also adds NCEP/CPC citation for the +0.78σ value.
